claim: Explorer evidence is valid: all project claims are backed by source-cited evidence, assumptions are properly separated, and the acceptance criteria are verified by test coverage.

evidence.summary: parseJsonCandidate exists and implements the described repair-on-fail logic with regex patterns for line ranges, escaping, and trailing commas
evidence.why: Verifies explorer claim about repair implementation

evidence.summary: normalizeAgentResult returns EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON error code when parsed is null, matching the fail-closed claim
evidence.why: Verifies the explicit blocker mechanism for unrecoverable JSON

evidence.summary: Final status logic sets blocked state when prd_ready is false, ensuring readiness stays blocked on invalid evidence
Test case 'repairs minor Claude JSON key drift' covers repair scenario and asserts json_repaired tracking
evidence.why: Verifies test coverage for recoverable drift acceptance criteria

evidence.summary: Test case 'invalid agent JSON fails closed with explicit blocker code' covers unrecoverable JSON and asserts EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON error code
evidence.why: Verifies test coverage for fail-closed acceptance criteria

risks: Assumption about regex coverage is reasonable but not proven for all drift variants; however, this is properly flagged as an assumption, not a fact.

result.notes: Explorer evidence is thorough and well-structured. All project claims cite exact paths and lines. Assumptions are clearly separated from facts. Test coverage directly validates acceptance criteria. No missing data. Ready to proceed.
